followupAnswerBank.question: What is transport testing strategy for CDS views?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Dev mein develop karo, QA transport karo, production se pehle fully test karo. Transport request mein sab objects hone chahiye: CDS view (DDLS), DCL object (DCLS), SQL view (SE11 entry), any dependent views. QA mein: functional test, authorization test, performance test different users se. Sign-off after QA — phir production transport.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS view transport strategy: Development transport includes DDLS (CDS source), DCLS (DCL role), and SE11 SQL view entry — all three must travel together. In QA, execute functional test (correct data returns), authorization test (DCL restricts correctly per user role), performance test (ST05 trace verifies execution plan acceptable), and regression test (existing consumers still work). Transport notes should document: what changed, test evidence, authorizations required. Production transport only after QA sign-off. For OData-exposed views, also activate service in /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE in each system.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Transport checklist we created after multiple incidents: 1) DDLS in transport, 2) DCLS in transport, 3) Any extended views in transport, 4) /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE activation documented, 5) QA test evidence attached (screenshots of F8 preview, /IWFND/GW_CLIENT test), 6) Authorization test evidence (tested with 3 user types), 7) ST05 trace confirming no performance regression. This checklist eliminated all CDS-related go-live issues for 6 months.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Sirf CDS view transport karna aur QA mein sirf admin user se test karna. Production mein regular users ko authorization error milta hai kyunki DCL transport miss hua ya end-user role testing nahi ki. Always transport complete set, always test with end-user equivalent roles.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Transport request contents for CDS view deployment:
" Check via SE10 or Eclipse Transport Organizer

" Required objects in transport:
" R3TR DDLS ZI_CUSTOMERORDER      ← CDS view
" R3TR DCLS ZI_CUSTOMERORDER      ← DCL role
" R3TR TABU  IWFND entries          ← if OData activation needed

" Post-transport checklist in QA:
" [ ] SE16N: data preview working?
" [ ] F8 in ADT: data correct?
" [ ] /IWFND/GW_CLIENT: OData working?
" [ ] Test with user having full auth: data visible?
" [ ] Test with restricted user: only permitted data?
" [ ] Test with no-auth user: error or empty?
" [ ] ST05 trace: single SQL, no N+1 pattern?
" [ ] Regression: existing reports still working?

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test CDS views that use input parameters?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Input parameters wale CDS views test karne ke liye parameter values pass karni padti hain. ADT F8 se parameter dialog automatically aata hai. ABAP program mein SELECT FROM view( param = value ) syntax use karo. Different parameter values test karo: valid value, invalid value, boundary values, empty/initial value — sab cases cover karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Testing parameterized CDS views: In ADT F8 preview, parameter dialog prompts for values — enter test values and execute. In ABAP test program, use SELECT FROM view_name( param1 = value1 ) syntax. Test scenarios: valid expected input (verify correct data), invalid input (verify no data or proper error), boundary values (date ranges, amounts at limits), initial/empty value (verify behavior — some views crash, some return all data). For OData, test parameters as function imports or as URL parameters depending on service definition.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: CDS view ZI_OpenItems had input parameter P_KeyDate. Test plan: P_KeyDate = '20241231' (year-end, expect many records), P_KeyDate = '19000101' (historical, expect zero records), P_KeyDate = sy-datum (current date, should match live open items), P_KeyDate = '99991231' (future date, should show all items). Each scenario verified data correctness. Found bug: initial date '00000000' caused HANA error — added ABAP wrapper that defaults to sy-datum if initial passed.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Sirf happy path test karna — valid parameter se data aata hai, done. Edge cases miss hote hain: what if date is in future? What if empty string passed? What if special characters? Parameterized views ka robust testing zaroori hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Testing parameterized CDS view
" ADT F8: dialog shows - Enter P_KeyDate: [__________]
" Type: 20241231 → Execute

" ABAP test for multiple parameter values
REPORT ztest_param_cds.

" Test case 1: Valid past date
SELECT * FROM zi_openitems( P_KeyDate = '20241231' )
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_yearend).
WRITE: / 'Year-end open items:', lines( lt_yearend ).

" Test case 2: Future date (should return all open items)
SELECT * FROM zi_openitems( P_KeyDate = '99991231' )
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_future).
WRITE: / 'Future date items:', lines( lt_future ).

" Test case 3: Very old date (should return 0)
SELECT * FROM zi_openitems( P_KeyDate = '20000101' )
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_old).
WRITE: / 'Old date items:', lines( lt_old ).

" Assert lt_yearend <= lt_future (future should include all)

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the role of /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE in CDS view testing?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE transaction OData services register aur activate karne ke liye hai. Jab tum @OData.publish: true wala CDS view activate karte ho, service automatically generate hoti hai lekin manually /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE mein register aur activate karni padti hai. Testing ke liye: service search karo, add to system, activate karo, phir /IWFND/GW_CLIENT se test karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE (OData Service Maintenance) is where generated OData services are registered and activated in a SAP system. After CDS view with @OData.publish: true is activated, the service exists in backend but is not accessible until registered here. Steps: open /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE, Add Service, search for auto-generated service name (usually CDS_VIEW_NAME_CDS), add it, activate it, test via /IWFND/GW_CLIENT button. Must be done in each system — Dev, QA, Prod — separately. The service URL format becomes /sap/opu/odata/sap/SERVICE_NAME/.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Typical Fiori project issue: Backend CDS view activated, OData service generated, but Fiori app showed 'Service not found'. Investigation: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE checked — service was created but not added and activated. Added service ZC_SALESORDER_CDS, activated, tested in /IWFND/GW_CLIENT — response 200 OK. Fiori app immediately worked. Classic setup step that new developers often skip.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Har system mein /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE activation karna bhool jana — transport ABAP objects carry karta hai but service activation is system-specific manual step (or scripted via SICF). In QA aur Production dono mein separately karna padta hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE steps:
" 1. Transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE
" 2. Click 'Add Service'
" 3. System Alias: LOCAL (or gateway alias)
" 4. Technical Service Name: search 'ZC_CUSTOMERORDER'
" 5. Find: ZC_CUSTOMERORDER_CDS (auto-generated name)
" 6. Select and Add
" 7. Activate ICF Node if prompted
" 8. Back to service list → Select service → Test
"    (opens /IWFND/GW_CLIENT automatically)

" Service URL after activation:
" https://host:port/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZC_CUSTOMERORDER_CDS/
" Test: append $metadata to verify structure
" https://host:port/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZC_CUSTOMERORDER_CDS/$metadata

cds-views-015.easyUnderstanding: Interviews mein CDS Views ke kuch tricky questions puche jaate hain jo confuse karte hain. 'CDS aur ALV mein kya difference hai?' — ye comparison hi galat hai, dono ka kaam alag hai. 'Kya CDS ABAP programs replace kar sakta hai?' — nahi, sirf data access layer replace karta hai. Annotation ke bare mein bhi kuch trap questions hain. Ye jaanna important hai ki interviewer kya sunna chahta hai.
cds-views-015.hinglishMasterExplanation:
CDS Views interview trap questions ko samajhna zaroori hai:

**Trap 1: 'CDS aur ALV mein kya difference hai?'**
Ye comparison hi wrong hai — CDS data layer hai, ALV presentation layer hai. Interviewer check karta hai ki tum layer confusion mein toh nahi ho. Sahi jawab: 'Ye comparison apple aur orange ka hai — dono alag purposes ke liye hain. CDS database se data access karta hai, ALV us data ko UI mein display karta hai.'

**Trap 2: 'Kya CDS Views ABAP programs ko replace kar sakte hain?'**
Nahi — CDS sirf data access/modeling layer replace karta hai. Business logic, complex processing, file operations, ALV output — sab ABAP mein hi rehta hai. CDS ABAP ke andar ka SELECT replace karta hai, poora ABAP nahi.

**Trap 3: '@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED — kab use karo?'**
#NOT_ALLOWED exist nahi karta — options hain: #CHECK, #NOT_REQUIRED, #CHECK_BUT_NO_RESTRICTION, #MANDATORY_CHECK. Interviewer test karta hai exact knowledge.

**Trap 4: 'CDS view SE11 table se kaisa alag hai?'**
SE11 table real data store karta hai, CDS view virtual definition hai — data access logic define karta hai, data store nahi karta. CDS is a VIEW, table is storage.

**Trap 5: 'Kya ek CDS view dusra CDS view use kar sakta hai?'**
Haan — ye CDS layering ka core concept hai. Interface view ko consumption view consume karta hai. CDS stacking supported aur recommended.

**Trap 6: 'DCL object kaunse system mein create hota hai?'**
Development system mein — same as CDS view, ADT mein, transport ke through promote hota hai. DCLS object type Eclipse ADT mein.

**Trap 7: 'CDS view mein WHERE clause aur HAVING mein kya difference hai?'**
WHERE non-aggregated rows filter karta hai, HAVING aggregated groups filter karta hai — same as standard SQL.
cds-views-015.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Interview mein CDS Views ke trap questions often wrong comparisons ya edge case knowledge test karte hain. Common trap: comparing CDS with ALV — these are different layers, not alternatives. CDS is the data access layer, ALV is the output presentation layer. Another trap: asking if CDS replaces ABAP — CDS replaces the data access patterns inside ABAP, not ABAP itself. Business logic, file operations, user interaction still need ABAP. For annotation traps: knowing exact annotation names and values matters — @AccessControl.authorizationCheck has specific allowed values: #CHECK, #NOT_REQUIRED, #CHECK_BUT_NO_RESTRICTION. Wrong values cause activation errors. For tricky DCL questions: DCL objects are ABAP Repository objects of type DCLS, created in ADT, transported same as CDS views, but are separate objects that must be explicitly included in transports.'

Hinglish tip: Trap questions ka best defense hai confident rehna aur agar nahi pata toh honestly bolna 'I am not sure about exact syntax, but the concept is...' Interviewer galat answer sunna nahi chahta — honest uncertainty better hai than wrong confident answer.
cds-views-015.codeExamples:
" Trap Q: What are valid values for @AccessControl.authorizationCheck?
" Answer - these are the valid values:
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK              " use DCL, check always
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED       " skip check (dangerous in prod)
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK_BUT_NO_RESTRICTION  " check but dont restrict

" Trap Q: Can a CDS view access another CDS view?
" Yes - this is normal layering:
define view ZC_ConsumptionView      " consumption view
  as select from ZI_InterfaceView   " uses another CDS view as source!
{ key ZI_InterfaceView.SalesOrder,
      ZI_InterfaceView.Customer }

" Trap Q: WHERE vs HAVING in CDS
define view ZI_AggregationDemo
  as select from vbak
{
  key vbak.kunnr as Customer,
  sum(vbak.netwr) as TotalValue
}
where vbak.audat >= '20240101'   " WHERE: filters before aggregation
group by vbak.kunnr
having sum(vbak.netwr) > 10000   " HAVING: filters after aggregation

" Trap Q: What is sqlViewName and is it mandatory?
" Old syntax: @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName required
" New syntax: DEFINE VIEW ENTITY - no sqlViewName needed
" Both are valid, newer projects prefer VIEW ENTITY

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: What is the difference between CDS Views and ALV?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ye question hi trap hai — CDS aur ALV ka direct comparison nahi hota. CDS data layer hai: database se data kaise fetch karo, join karo, aggregate karo ye define karta hai. ALV presentation layer hai: ABAP program jo data screen pe kaise dikhaye. Dono saath use hote hain: CDS se data lao, ALV mein dikhao. Replace nahi karte ek doosre ko.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS Views and ALV operate at completely different layers and are not alternatives to each other. CDS is a data access layer — it defines how data is fetched, joined, and modeled at the database level. ALV (ABAP List Viewer) is a presentation framework — it displays tabular data in ABAP programs with features like sorting, filtering, column selection. They are used together: SELECT data FROM a CDS view into an internal table, then display that internal table using ALV. CDS replaces raw SQL patterns; ALV is the output mechanism.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Project usage together: Inventory report uses ZI_InventoryStatus CDS view (joins MARA, MARD, MARC — material stock data). ABAP report selects data from CDS view into internal table. ALV displays the internal table with sorting by plant, color coding for low stock. CDS handled data model, ALV handled display. Neither replaces the other.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Interview mein ye answer dena ki 'CDS views ne ALV replace kar diya.' Ye completely wrong hai. CDS ne SELECT * FROM mara type raw SQL coding replace kiya hai — aur Fiori apps ke liye ALV ki zarurat nahi — lekin classical ABAP reports mein ALV aaj bhi standard hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" CDS + ALV together - not alternatives
REPORT zrep_cds_with_alv.

" Step 1: Fetch data via CDS view (data layer)
SELECT matnr, mtart, material_desc
  FROM zcds_material_desc  " CDS view does the join
  WHERE mtart = 'FERT'
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_materials).

" Step 2: Display via ALV (presentation layer)
DATA(lo_alv) = cl_salv_table=>factory(
  IMPORTING r_salv_table = DATA(lo_table)
  CHANGING  t_table      = lt_materials ).
lo_table->display( ).  " ALV renders the output
" CDS = data, ALV = display — complementary layers

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: Can CDS Views replace ABAP programs completely?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Nahi — ye common misconception hai. CDS sirf data access replace karta hai — joins, views, OData exposure. ABAP ki zarurat hoti hai: complex business logic, file I/O, user dialogs, email sending, BAPIs calling, complex output formatting. CDS ek tool hai ABAP toolbox mein — replacement nahi. 'CDS ne ABAP replace kiya' — ye answer galat hai, interviewer trap mein phansa raha hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS Views replace only the data access and modeling layer — they do not replace ABAP programs. ABAP is still needed for: complex business logic processing, procedural workflows, file operations, spool output, form printing, calling external systems, complex user interaction via dialog programs, BAPIs, RFCs, background jobs, and much more. CDS replaces: raw SELECT statements, SE11 database views, some SEGW OData work. For Fiori apps, CDS + RAP can replace some classical ABAP, but complex custom logic still needs ABAP classes or function modules.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Customer question during training: 'If we use CDS for everything, can we eliminate ABAP?' Reality check: CDS-based Fiori app for purchase order creation uses CDS view for data display, but the actual save logic — validation, posting to EKKO/EKPO, workflow trigger, email notification, error handling — still requires ABAP (RAP behavior implementation). CDS is the data model, ABAP is the logic. You cannot send an email from a CDS view annotation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Interview mein zyada modernism dikhane ke chakkar mein ye bolna 'CDS ne ABAP replace kar diya in S/4HANA.' Interviewer immediately follow-up puche ga: 'Toh business logic kahan jaati hai?' Correct answer: 'CDS data access layer modernize karta hai, ABAP business logic layer pe rehta hai.'
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" What CDS replaces:
" OLD: SELECT * FROM mara INTO TABLE @lt_mat. ← raw SQL
" NEW: SELECT * FROM zi_materialdata INTO TABLE @lt_mat. ← CDS

" What CDS cannot replace (still needs ABAP):
" 1. Business logic
IF ls_order-netwr > 100000.  " ABAP condition
  CALL FUNCTION 'CREDIT_CHECK'.  " ABAP FM call
ENDIF.
" 2. File operations - only ABAP
OPEN DATASET '/data/file.csv' FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE.
" 3. Email sending - only ABAP
DATA(lo_mail) = cl_bcs=>create_instance( ).
" 4. Complex output - ABAP + SmartForms/Adobe Forms
" CDS = data model only, not program replacement

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: What is the exact valid list of @AccessControl.authorizationCheck values?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Interviewer exact annotation values poochh sakta hai — ek wrong value bolne se trap mein phans jaate ho. Valid values hain: #CHECK (DCL se check karo), #NOT_REQUIRED (check mat karo — dev only), #CHECK_BUT_NO_RESTRICTION (check karo but restrict mat karo — for testing). #NOT_ALLOWED, #DISABLED, #NONE — ye values exist nahi karti. Koi bhi wrong value bolna compilation error deta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The valid values for @AccessControl.authorizationCheck annotation are: #CHECK — authorization is checked using DCL role, recommended for production. #NOT_REQUIRED — no authorization check performed, only for development/testing, never production. #CHECK_BUT_NO_RESTRICTION — authorization object is checked for syntax but no data restriction applied, used for testing DCL structure without actual restriction. There is no #NOT_ALLOWED, #NONE, or #DISABLED value — these would cause CDS activation errors. Default when annotation is omitted is #CHECK.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Interview question: Candidate said @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #DISABLED is used for development. Interviewer asked to confirm — candidate doubled down. Interviewer tested: #DISABLED does not exist, activation error occurs. Candidate failed technical screen. Knowing exact valid values demonstrates real hands-on CDS experience. Easy to verify: Eclipse ADT content assist (Ctrl+Space) after #  shows valid enum values.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Annotation values guess karna ya remember karne ki koshish karna bina verify kiye. ADT mein Ctrl+Space content assist sahi values dikhata hai. Bina ADT ke: SAP Help documentation mein @AccessControl annotation page dekho. Wrong annotation values = immediate activation error = view does not work.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Valid @AccessControl.authorizationCheck values:

" #CHECK - standard production value
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
define view ZI_ProductionView ...
" Requires: DCL source DCLS object with same name

" #NOT_REQUIRED - dev/testing only
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
define view ZI_TestView ...
" No DCL needed, no auth check - NEVER in production!

" #CHECK_BUT_NO_RESTRICTION - testing DCL syntax
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK_BUT_NO_RESTRICTION
define view ZI_TestRestrictedView ...
" DCL checked for errors but not enforced

" INVALID - these do not exist (activation error!):
" @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #DISABLED  ← ERROR!
" @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NONE       ← ERROR!
" @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #OFF        ← ERROR!

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: What is the difference between a CDS view and a CDS view entity?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: DEFINE VIEW — purana syntax, SQL view name jaroori hai (@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName). DEFINE VIEW ENTITY — naya syntax S/4HANA ABAP 7.55+, SQL view name nahi chahiye, newer features support karta hai jaise abstract entities aur draft handling. Interview mein both ka knowledge dikhao — older projects mein DEFINE VIEW milega, newer projects mein VIEW ENTITY.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: DEFINE VIEW is the original CDS syntax requiring @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName annotation — this creates both a CDS view and an underlying SE11 SQL view. DEFINE VIEW ENTITY is the newer syntax (ABAP 7.55+) that does not require sqlViewName — it is a pure ABAP-layer object without a corresponding SE11 SQL view. View entities support newer features like abstract entities, draft entities for RAP, and have slightly different behavior with SELECT. SAP recommends VIEW ENTITY for new development in S/4HANA 2022+.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Migration project for new S/4HANA 2023 implementation: Architect decision — all new CDS views use DEFINE VIEW ENTITY. Team initially confused why no sqlViewName annotation. Explained: VIEW ENTITY is the modern approach, no SE11 SQL view created, more aligned with RAP framework, draft handling built-in. Old views from 2019 implementation used DEFINE VIEW — both coexist in same system. Forward path is VIEW ENTITY.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: 'Dono same hain — sirf syntax alag hai' kehna. VIEW ENTITY has real differences: no SQL view in SE11, different behavior in some SELECT scenarios, RAP draft support, and slightly different activation path. Not just syntax sugar.

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: How do associations work in CDS — are they JOINs or not?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ye tricky question hai. Associations JOINs nahi hain by default — ye lazy relationships hain jo tabhi resolve hoti hain jab explicitly path expression se access karo. Lekin agar tum association ka field seedha SELECT list mein use karo (e.g., _Customer.name1 as CustomerName), tab JOIN ho jaata hai. Agar sirf association expose karo consumers ke liye, tab no join by default. Ye nuance jaanna interviewer ko impress karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Associations are not JOINs by default — they are declared relationships resolved lazily. If the association is only exposed to consumers by listing the association name in the SELECT list (e.g., _Customer), no join occurs unless the consumer explicitly accesses it using path expression. However, if association fields are directly projected in the view's SELECT list (e.g., _Customer.name1 as CustomerName), then a join is executed when the view is queried — the association becomes an implicit join. Best practice: expose associations to consumers for lazy resolution; only project association fields when always needed.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Performance investigation: CDS view had _Customer association. Developer added _Customer.name1 directly to SELECT list — every query now always joined KNA1 even when only order header was needed. Split into: ZI_OrderHeader (association exposed, not projected) and ZC_OrderWithCustomer (consumption view that projects _Customer.name1 for Fiori app that always shows customer name). Header report performance improved 3x — KNA1 join removed for pure header queries.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: 'Association matlab JOIN nahi hota kabhi' kehna. Ye half-truth hai. When you project association's fields directly in SELECT list, join happens. Association stays lazy only when exposed as navigation property to consumer. This distinction matters for performance architecture.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Case 1: Association exposed - NO join by default
define view ZI_OrderLazy
  as select from vbak
  association [0..1] to kna1 as _Customer
    on $projection.kunnr = _Customer.kunnr
{
  key vbak.vbeln,
      vbak.kunnr,
      _Customer  " exposed as nav property - NO join unless consumer uses it
}
" Consumer: SELECT vbeln, kunnr FROM zi_orderlazy → NO join to KNA1
" Consumer: SELECT vbeln, _Customer.name1 FROM zi_orderlazy → JOIN to KNA1

" Case 2: Association fields projected - JOIN always happens
define view ZI_OrderEager
  as select from vbak
  association [0..1] to kna1 as _Customer
    on $projection.kunnr = _Customer.kunnr
{
  key vbak.vbeln,
      vbak.kunnr,
      _Customer.name1 as CustomerName  " projected! JOIN always executed
}

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: What happens if two CDS views have the same @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: sqlViewName unique hona chahiye poori SAP system mein — table names jaisa. Agar do CDS views same sqlViewName use karein, second view activate hi nahi hoga — activation error aayega. SQLViewName effectively ek SE11 view create karta hai — duplicate SE11 objects allowed nahi hain. Naming conventions follow karo: ZI_SALESORD_V, ZC_SALESORD_V — distinct names.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName value must be globally unique within the SAP system — it represents an SE11 database view object name. If two CDS views specify the same sqlViewName, the second view will fail activation with an error indicating duplicate SE11 view name. This is similar to two tables with the same name. Naming convention recommendation: derive sqlViewName from CDS view name — take CDS view name, apply abbreviation rules to fit within 16 characters, ensure uniqueness. Example: CDS view ZI_CustomerSalesOrder_2024 → sqlViewName ZCUSTSALORD24V.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Team incident: Two developers created CDS views in parallel — ZI_MaterialData and ZI_MaterialDesc — and both used sqlViewName 'ZMATDATA_V'. When second developer tried to activate, error: 'ZMATDATA_V already exists as SE11 view'. Resolution: one renamed sqlViewName to 'ZMATDESC_V'. Lesson: communicate naming within team, maintain a naming registry for sqlViewName values to avoid conflicts.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: CDS view ko copy-paste karke new view banana aur sqlViewName change karna bhool jana. Two views same sqlViewName = one of them never activates. Always change BOTH the DEFINE VIEW name AND the @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName when copying.

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: Can you call a function module or method inside a CDS view?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Nahi — ye CDS Views ka hard limitation hai aur bahut common trap question hai. CDS Views sirf SQL-compatible operations support karte hain: SELECT, JOIN, CASE, built-in SQL functions. ABAP function modules, methods, classes — kuch bhi call nahi kar sakte CDS mein. Agar complex logic chahiye jo FM ya method se ho, woh ABAP layer mein karo CDS view consume karne ke baad.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS Views do not support calling ABAP function modules, methods, or classes — they are SQL-based data definitions, not ABAP programs. This is a fundamental architectural constraint. CDS views can use: SQL built-in functions (CONCAT, SUBSTRING, CAST, UPPER, LOWER, COALESCE, CASE expressions), arithmetic operations, aggregate functions. They cannot call: BAPI, FM, ABAP class methods, user-defined functions. If complex non-SQL logic is needed, the pattern is: CDS view provides raw data, ABAP class/program consumes CDS view and applies business logic.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Design discussion: Business wanted CDS view to calculate 'preferred vendor score' using a complex algorithm. Developer suggested calling Z_CALC_VENDOR_SCORE FM from within CDS view. Not possible — CDS is SQL only. Solution: CDS view ZI_VendorBase provides raw vendor data. ABAP class ZCL_VendorScore calculates score: selects from ZI_VendorBase, applies scoring algorithm, stores in Z-table ZVENDOR_SCORE. Second CDS view ZI_VendorWithScore reads ZI_VendorBase and ZVENDOR_SCORE — joins via CDS. Two-step approach: logic in ABAP, result accessible via CDS.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: 'Main ek CASE statement se complex logic handle kar lunga CDS mein' sochna — CASE works for simple conditions but not procedural logic. FM calls, database commits, file reads, BAPI calls — none possible in CDS. Know the boundary: CDS = declarative SQL, ABAP = procedural logic.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" IMPOSSIBLE in CDS - no FM/method calls
" This does NOT work:
define view ZI_VendorScore_WRONG
  as select from lfa1
{
  key lfa1.lifnr,
  " CALL FUNCTION 'Z_CALC_SCORE'  ← SYNTAX ERROR in CDS!
  " ZCL_SCORE=>calculate( lifnr ) ← NOT POSSIBLE in CDS!
  lfa1.name1
}

" CORRECT pattern - ABAP does logic, CDS provides data
" Step 1: CDS provides raw data
define view ZI_VendorBase
  as select from lfa1
{ key lifnr, name1, land1 }

" Step 2: ABAP class calculates score, stores in Z-table
" ZCL_VENDOR_SCORER reads ZI_VendorBase, calculates, updates ZVSCORE

" Step 3: CDS joins base + score result
define view ZI_VendorWithScore
  as select from ZI_VendorBase
  left outer join zvscore on ZI_VendorBase.lifnr = zvscore.lifnr
{ key lifnr, name1, zvscore.score as VendorScore }

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: What is the difference between CDS EXTEND VIEW and inheritance in OO ABAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CDS EXTEND VIEW aur ABAP class inheritance dono different cheezein hain. EXTEND VIEW ek existing CDS view mein additional fields add karta hai bina original view modify kiye — jaise ek enhancement. ABAP inheritance child class mein parent behavior override karna hai. CDS mein koi override nahi hota — sirf field addition. Dono mein 'extension' word similar hai lekin concept bilkul different hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS EXTEND VIEW adds fields to an existing CDS view without modifying the base view — it is an additive extension, not inheritance. You cannot override existing fields, change their types, or alter the view's logic. It is used to add custom fields (from append structures) to SAP standard views in an upgrade-safe way. ABAP OO inheritance allows method overriding, polymorphism, and behavior modification. CDS extension is purely additive — fields only. There is no equivalent of method overriding in CDS; you cannot change how existing fields are calculated.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Customer requirement: Add custom field ZZPAYMENT_TERMS_TEXT to SAP standard I_SalesOrder view. Solution: EXTEND VIEW I_SalesOrder WITH ZX_SalesOrder_Payment — added ZZPAYMENT_TERMS_TEXT field from VBAK append structure. I_SalesOrder now shows custom field in all consumers. Not a modification — upgrade-safe extension. Could not change existing SalesOrder or Customer fields — only addition allowed. This is the correct extension approach for standard SAP CDS views.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: EXTEND VIEW se existing field logic change karne ki koshish karna — ye possible nahi hai. Agar existing field ka calculation change karna ho, consume that view in a new ZI_ custom view and override the field with new alias or CASE. Extension = add only, not modify.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" CDS EXTEND VIEW - additive fields only
extend view I_SalesOrder with ZX_SalesOrder_Custom
{
  " Adding custom fields from VBAK append structure
  vbak.zzpayment_notes    as PaymentNotes,
  vbak.zzregion_custom    as SalesRegion
  " Cannot change existing fields like SalesOrder, Customer
  " Cannot override calculation of existing fields
  " Only new field addition allowed
}

" vs ABAP OO inheritance - can override
CLASS zcl_child DEFINITION INHERITING FROM zcl_parent.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS get_name REDEFINITION.  " override parent method
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_child IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_name.
    " Different implementation than parent
    result = 'Custom Name'.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
" CDS EXTEND = additive only; OO inheritance = override possible
